evals.name: product-reviews-embed-vs-reference
evals.prompt: I'm building an e-commerce platform and I'm stuck on how to model product reviews. Each product can have anywhere from 0 to maybe 5000 reviews (most products have under 100 though). Each review has: reviewer name, rating (1-5 stars), review text (up to 2000 chars), helpful votes count, and timestamp. On the product page, I show the product details plus the 10 most recent reviews, and there's a "see all reviews" link that takes you to a paginated list. I also have a separate "My Reviews" page where users can see all their own reviews across all products. Should I embed the reviews in the product document or keep them in a separate reviews collection? I'm worried about the 16MB limit but also don't want to do unnecessary lookups.
evals.expected_output: A clear recommendation on whether to embed or reference, with specific reasoning about: 1) Access patterns (reviews shown with product vs independently), 2) Cardinality (unbounded growth potential with popular products), 3) Document size concerns (5000 reviews would exceed 16MB), 4) Query patterns (pagination, user's reviews across products). Should recommend a hybrid or reference approach given the unbounded nature and independent access patterns.

evals.name: blog-comments-outlier-pattern
evals.prompt: I'm modeling blog posts and comments. Most posts have 5-20 comments, but a few viral posts have 50,000+ comments. Should I embed the comments in the post document or use a separate collection?
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this as an outlier scenario and suggest the Outlier Pattern: embed comments for normal posts (5-20 comments) for performance, but use a separate collection or overflow strategy for viral posts that exceed a threshold. Pure separation works but misses optimization opportunity for the common case (95%+ of posts).

evals.name: product-view-counter
evals.prompt: I'm building a product catalog for a high-traffic e-commerce site. Each product document has: name, description, price, category, SKU, inventory count, viewCount, and tags. Any schema concerns I should be aware of?
evals.expected_output: Should recognize the viewCount field as a high-frequency counter problem. Should ask about how often viewCount is updated or recognize that incrementing on every page view creates massive write contention and rewrites the entire product document constantly. Should recommend the Approximation Pattern (pattern-approximation.md) - update counter occasionally rather than every view, or use a separate collection/field for high-frequency counters. This is 'different update frequencies' in action: product data is mostly stable, but viewCount is updated constantly. May also mention inventory count if it updates frequently, but viewCount is the primary issue.

assertions.name: identifies_high_frequency_field
assertions.description: Asks about update frequency for viewCount or other fields, OR recognizes viewCount as a high-frequency counter without being told

assertions.name: mentions_approximation_pattern
assertions.description: Recommends or mentions the Approximation Pattern (update counter occasionally, not on every increment)

assertions.name: mentions_write_contention_or_performance
assertions.description: Mentions write contention, document rewrites, performance impact, or the cost of frequent updates to the product document

assertions.name: references_different_update_frequencies
assertions.description: Identifies that different fields have different update frequencies (stable product data vs hot viewCount) as a key schema concern

assertions.name: avoids_naive_approval
assertions.description: Should NOT say the schema looks fine without addressing the viewCount high-frequency update issue
